properties.remark: Several trees down in the Cobbtown area, near Highway 23.

A National Weather Service storm survey team comfirmed an EF1 tornado with peak winds of 100 MPH in southwest Evans County. The tornado had a width of 200 yards and length of 4.78 miles. Damage consisted primarily of tree damage, including hundreds of trees that were either uprooted or snapped off. One small shed was mostly destroyed. A center pivot irrigation system was also destroyed.

NWS Storm Survey team estimated peak wind gusts of 80 to 85 mph across the Summerville and Ladson areas. A swath of damaging winds measured 8.5 miles in length and 3.5 miles in width. Numerous homes and vehicles were significantly damaged by uprooted or snapped trees. Additionally, some minor damage to residential property was observed, including shingles and siding blown off and privacy fences blown down.

*** 1 Inj *** Berkeley County EM records indicated at least 28 homes damaged by falling trees. One female reported injured when a tree fell on her residence on College Park Drive. In addition, there were at least 18 reported downed trees and powerlines. A pole barn was also significantly damaged off of Cindy Drive.

NWS Storm Survey found that the tornado on Isle of Palms had a damage path of 0.72 miles and path width of 75 yards. The estimated peak wind was 87 mph, or EF1. This tornado occurred with Tropical Storm Debby.

NWS Storm Survey indicated that one of two tornadoes on Edisto Beach had a damage path of 2.89 miles and a path width of 300 yards. Estimated peak wind was 97 mph, or EF1. This tornado occurred with Tropical Storm Debby.
Colleton County Emergency Manager reported a sighted tornado on the 100 block of jungle road.
Colleton County Emergency Manager reported a tornado sighted and a tree on a building on 133 Palmetto Blvd.

Colleton County Emergency Manager reported a tornado sighted on 101 Palmetto Blvd. Also, reported multiple trees down, power lines down, debris on roadways, and flooded roadways.
Charleston County Dispatch reported a sighted tornado going over a residence's home on the 500 block of Fishing Creek Drive. Damage to the residence's porch was reported.
Colleton County Emergency Manager reported a sighted tornado on 2700 block of Palmetto Blvd.
Social Media reported trees downs at Burroughs Hall at Govenors Drive due to Tropical Storm Debby.
Charleston County consolidated dispatch relayed a report of a sighted tornado touchdown on Outback Lane off Palmetto Rd on Edisto Island. Numerous trees down.
Colleton County Emergency Manager reported a tornado sighted on 101 Palmetto Blvd. Also, reported multiple trees down, power lines down, debris on roadways, and flooded roadways.

NWS Storm Survey indicated that the second tornado on Edisto Island had a damage path of 0.56 miles and width of 100 yards. Estimate peak wind was 90 mph, or EF1. This tornado occurred with Tropical Storm Debby.

NWS Storm Survey indicated that the tornado on Kiawah Island had a damage path of 1.18 miles and width of 150 yards. Estimated peak wind of 85 mph, or EF0. This tornado occurred with Tropical Storm Debby.
Social Media reported trees downs at Burroughs Hall at Govenors Drive due to Tropical Storm Debby.
Public reported a couple of big trees down in their backayard at the 4700 block of River Road due to Tropical Storm Debby.
Media relayed a picture of a couple of trees down on 490 Sams Point Road due to Tropical Storm Debby.

NWS Storm Survey indicated that the tornado in West Ashley had a damage path of 3.88 miles and width of 200 yards. The estimated peak wind was 77 mph, or EF0. This tornado occurred with Tropical Storm Debby.

NWS storm survey found that the tornado on St. Helena Island had a damage path of 0.85 miles and width of 60 yards. Estimated peak wind was 74 mph, or EF0. This tornado occurred with Tropical Storm Debby.

NWS Storm Survey indicated that the tornado on Lady's Island had a damage path of 2.14 miles and path width of 60 yards. The estimated peak wind was 85 mph, or EF0. This tornado occurred with Tropical Storm Debby.

The Evans County Emergency Manager provided drone video of a breach of a dam on Smith Brothers lake which is located just west of Perry Road and south of Edgar Griffin Road. This is an 85 acre lake that is overflowing and Perry Road is closed and not safe for travel. The overtopping started in the early morning hours.

NWS Storm Survey indicated that the tornado near Moncks Corner had a damage path of 3.88 miles and width of 150 yards. The estimated peak wind speed was 95 mph, or EF1. This tornado occurred with Tropical Storm Debby.
Berkeley County EM reported a tornado on the eastern side of Moncks Corner. An Applebees was damaged. Several trees and power poles were blown down, some onto cars. At least one car was flipped over.

Berkeley County EM reported a tornado on the eastern side of Moncks Corner. An Applebees was damaged. Several trees and power poles were blown down, some onto cars. At least one car was flipped over. Significant damage to an Arby's was reported (windows shattered and parts of the roof torn off). A truck was flipped over on its side.

Berkeley County EM reported a tornado on the eastern side of Moncks Corner. An Applebees was damaged. Several trees and power poles were blown down, some onto cars. At least one car was flipped over. Significant damage to an Arby's was reported (windows shattered and parts of the roof torn off). A truck was flipped over on its side. A Walmart was damaged (front windows shattered and lots of overturned shopping carts).
Social media video shows a tornado tracking across a portion of a car lot near the intersection of North US Highway 52 and Rembert Dennis Blvd.
Flood waters have entered several businesses along Ashley River Road near Otis Drive.
Water has entered the back of several homes near Ashley Hall Road and St. Julian Drive.
Road washed out around the intersection of Cane Branch Rd and Edward Rd.
EM reported two homes flooded along Willard Street in the town of Hampton, SC. In addition, several roads were washed out or flooded across the county.
Charleston Fire Station 8 on Huger Street reports several inches of floodwater entered their station on Tuesday morning.

The water level on Church Creek near West Ashley was 6.51 ft and rising, moderate flood stage is 6.5 ft. At 6.5 ft, water from the creek will commonly impact Crosstowne Church. As Church Creek continues to rise, water could also impact homes on Wolk Drive and Two Loch Place.
Emergency Manager reports structure flooded on Corvair Ave in Savannah GA.
A portion of Roadway damaged in the 2500 block of Black Creek Road.
A portion of Roadway damaged near the intersection of Black Creek Road and Edward Road.
Fire officials reported flood waters entered a home at the intersection of Farm Lake Drive and Cattle Run Way. Waters were also about to enter several homes nearby.
A portion of Roadway damaged in the 600 block of Cedar Creek Road.
Bennetts Point Road near the intersection of Island Flat Lane has been closed due to deep flooding. The entire area is impassable.
A portion of Roadway damaged in the 8300 block of Bennetts Point Road.
A portion of Roadway damaged in the 100 block of Good Hope Landing Lane.
Savannah Emergency Manager reported flood waters entered a home in the 300 block of Comet Street. Water was just about to enter several nearby homes.

Emergency Manager reported around 10 roads washed out across the county. The washed out road were listed in the following locations: 1100 block of Aensdorff Lane, Springfield-Egypt Road and Tarver Pond Drive and Turkey Branch Road, Lorenzo Hurst Road, Old Louisville Road and Riverside Drive, Herbert Kessler Road and Blue Jay Road, Corinth Church Road. The 300 block of South Ash Street was washed out and road bed was washed away.
Flowing water was seen entering buildings near the intersection of Furman Lane and Oxford Road.

The City of Charleston reported over 60 roads closed across the city due to flooding. City of Charleston web page indicates that all primary access points to the Charleston peninsula have been closed to incoming traffic due to widespread flooding.

EM reported a water rescue in progress on Magwood Drive. Another resident on the 9000 block of Dorchester Road requested assistance. In addition, a guardrail along Dorchester Road was washed out, water along the road was bankfull.
37 homes surrounded by flood waters in the Tremont Park area. Flood waters have entered 6 homes.
The river gauge at French Quarter Creek near Huger reached the moderate flood stage of 11.5 ft. This is the level where flash flooding begins along the creek.
Langfordville Road collapsed over Beaverdam Branch, washing a car and occupant downstream. An adult female was rescued from the floodwaters without injury.
Flood water has entered one home and surrounded another home along Bay Pine Drive.
A portion of Roadway washed out near the intersection of Mill Pond Road and Low Country Highway.
A portion of Roadway washed out in the 400 block of Paradise Road.
Berkeley County Emergency Management reports a washout of Cane Gulley Road near Highway 402.
A portion of Roadway washed out in the 600 block of Wiggins Road.
A road washout was reported at 5000 Wiggins Rd.
Dorchester County Emergency Management reported that the Dept of Transportation was shutting down Delmar Highway at Ashley Ridge High School because the roadway was washing away due to flood waters.
A portion of Roadway damaged in the 2400 block of Mewville Road.
Emergency manager reported the collapse of a section of Levi Kennedy Road near Hodges Pond due to flood waters.
Emergency manager reported a road collapse along Harmony Church Road due to flood waters.
Emergency manager reported a collapsed paved road along a section of Cedar Hall Road by the Big Club House Pond caused by flood waters.
Emergency manager reported Joe Waters Road was washed out due to flood waters leading to a vehicle being washed down the creek. Assistance was needed for the occupant with no injuries reported.

At least 25 roads are washed out across Jenkins County due to heavy rainfall from Debby. Most of these roads were listed on the Jenkins County Board of Commissioners Facebook page at 721 PM EDT on 8/7. Event times for each individual road wash out are unknown but likely occurred during the day of 8/6 or overnight into early 8/7.
Emergency manager reports water evacuation in progress along River Oak Dr due to flooding along the Ogeechee River near Eden.
Emergency manager reports evacuations in progress along Edgewater Dr due to flooding along the Ogeechee River.

Colleton County Emergency Management reported that the drainage pipe under the roadway in the 1300 block of Forks Road has washed out due to heavy rainfall from Debby. The exact date and time of the event is unknown.
Colleton County Emergency Management reported a tree down on Longleaf Drive near Jefferies Highway.
Colleton County Emergency Management reported a tree down and power lines down blocking one lane near the intersection of Wesley Grove Road and Rehoboth Road.
Colleton County Emergency Management reported a tree down near the intersection of Round O Road and Sidneys Road. The tree was blocking both lanes of the roadway.
A video was relayed of a flooded house on Fairlawn Drive in Moncks Corner.
Emergency officials in Moncks Corner reported that high water rescues are in progress. Homes are flooded throughout the city and roads are closed due to 2-3 feet of fast moving water.
Emergency management reported approximately 30 swift water rescues occurred within Berkeley County, mainly in Moncks Corner. No injuries have been reported.
Emegency Manager reported a tree blocking the Haynesville Ext due to flood flashing and thunderstorm wind.
Emegency Manager reported a tree blocking the Haynesville Ext due to flood flashing and thunderstorm wind.
A picture on social media shows a stranded car in flood waters located on Nexton Pkwy and Pine Hollow Dr. Two occupants were rescued from vehicle with no reported injuries.
Emergency manager shared a picture of paved Wire Road washed out near the intersection of Zion Road.

Emergency manager and media reported at least 75 roads closed due to flooding across Dorchester County this morning. A slightly larger amount of closures seemed to be near Harleyville, Summerville, and along the Edisto River.
A video posted on social media shows water rapidly flowing across a commercial parking lot and across a boat ramp near Reid Hill Rd.
Emergency manager shared a picture of the paved 695 block of Powder Horn Road washed out.
Emergency manager relayed an image of water which looks to be moving over Salem Rd where it intersects with Halfway Gut Creek.
Emergency manager indicated paved roadway damage on Summers Drive between 17A and Victoria Point. A small bridge in between these locations is starting to crumble at this time.
Emergency manager reported a road closure along Charleston Hwy between Neyles and Jacksonboro at Horseshoe Creek bridge due to flooding. Water is actively flowing over the roadway.
Emergency management reported approximately 30 swift water rescues occurred earlier today within Berkeley County, mainly in Moncks Corner. No injuries have been reported.
Berkeley County Emergency Mangement reported Heatly Street in Moncks Corner is being closed due to high water caused by flash flooding.
Emergency manager reports tree down along Augusta Hwy before Peirce Rd due to thunderstorm outflow.
Patriot's Point Fire Division reported a waterspout in Charleston Harbor near Castle Pinckney.
Tree reported down on Statesboro Highway at Dover Road. Time estimated from radar.
Tree reported down on Jarrell Pond Road near Stoney Pond Road. Time estimated from radar.
Tree reported down on Clito Road at Franklin Road. Time estimated from radar.
Tree reported down on South Wynn Road at Shaw Road. Time estimated from radar.

The NOS tide gauge at Fort Pulaski measured a 55 knot wind gust.
The NOS tide gauge at Fort Pulaski measured a 44 knot wind gust.
A resident on Eagles Nest Drive reported a large Oak tree down on the 120 block. Time determined by nearby observations.

The Weatherflow site on the north end of Tybee Island measured a 46 knot wind gust.
The Weatherflow site on the south end of Tybee Island measured a 57 knot wind gust
The Weatherflow site in Calibogue Sound measured a 54 knot wind gust.

Emergency manager reported 1 tree blocking roadway in 200 block of Salem Road, and a few large branches down on roadway in that area.
EM reported several trees down in the Bonneau area. Time estimated from radar data.
Buoy 41008 measured a 37 knot wind gust.
Buoy 41008 measured a peak wind gust of 49 knots.
The Weatherflow site at Shutes Folly in Charleston Harbor measured a 44 knot wind gust.
The NOS station at the Charleston Pilot Boat office measured a 36 knot wind gusts.
The Weatherflow site located at Savannah Entrance Light measured a 35 knot wind gust.
The Weatherflow site located on the north end of Tybee Island measured a 45 knot wind gust.

An EF-0 tornado with max winds of 80 mph touched down on Daufuskie Island near Drivewood Cottage Lane. The tornado moved northward across the island and lifted just north of Big House Plantation Road near Myrtle Island on the mainland.

An EF-0 tornado with max winds of 80 mph touched down on Daufuskie Island near Drivewood Cottage Lane. The tornado moved northward across the island and lifted just north of Big House Plantation Road near Myrtle Island on the mainland. Path length was 6.66 miles and the path width was 150 yards.
Emergency Manager relayed report of trees snapped on Stoddard Court and also minor roof damage on the 20 block of Martinangel Lane. Possible tornado.
Pictures relayed of several trees down on Martinangel Lane near Captain Monroe Lane on Daufuskie Island. Possible tornado.
Report relayed of a tree snapped and minor debris on Game Land Road. Possible tornado.

An EF-0 tornado with max winds of 76 mph touched down near Headwaters Road in Palmetto Bluff near Pritchardville. The tornado moved north and crossed the far western marshy area of the May River before crossing May River Road. The tornado dissipated just to the north of May River Road.

An EF-0 tornado with max winds of 76 mph touched down near Headwaters Road in Palmetto Bluff near Pritchardville. The tornado moved north and crossed the far western marshy area of the May River before crossing May River Road. The tornado dissipated just to the north of May River Road. Path length was 1.27 miles with a path width of 100 yards.
Beaufort County Sheriff's Office reported property damage from a tornado on Driftwood Drive on Daufuskie Island.
Beaufort County Sheriff's Office reported a tree down from a tornado on Palmetto Bluff in Pritchardville.

An EF-1 tornado with max winds around 90 mph touched down near Gabriel Road in Colleton County, SC and lifted just north of Ehrhardt near Baucham Road in Bamberg County, SC. Path length was 7.81 miles with a path width of 150 yards.
Emergency manager relayed report of power lines down at Bells Highway and High Cotton Road. Possible tornado.
Emergency manager relayed report of tree down at Bells Highway and Woodrow Drive. Possible tornado.
Tree down blocking both lanes on 22000 block of Bells Highway. Possible tornado.
Emergency manager relayed report of trees down on Bells Highway near the Bamberg county border. Possible tornado.
Dorchester County Emergency Management reported a tree down on Highway 15 between Grover and the Edisto River.

An EF-0 tornado with max winds near 70 mph impacted the Gardens Corner area. The tornado began just southeast of Washington Farm Road then traveled northwest across Stroban Road near Daniels Drive. The tornado then crossed US Highway 17 before dissipating in a marshy area. Path length was 3.87 miles with a path width of 175 yards.

An EF-0 tornado with max winds of 80 mph touched down along Bonanza Rd near Savannah Hwy, moved through parts of the Boltons Landing subdivision and lifted at Saint Hubert Way just off Bees Ferry Road. Path length was 2.02 miles with a path width of 90 yards.

AN EF-0 tornado with max winds of 80 mph touched down near Bentz Road and traveled north northwest and crossed Liberia Road and Katy Hill Road before dissipating just south of Bears Bluff Road. Path length was 2.09 miles and the path width was 125 yards.

An EF-0 tornado with max winds of 70 mph touched down near Savannah Highway and Davidson Road, then moved northwest and lifted near Bulow Landing Road. Path length was 1.96 miles with a path length of 90 yards.

An EF-1 tornado with max winds of 90 mph first produce damage along Brigger Hill Road and McCants Road. The tornado moved north across Leadenwah Drive and Allendale Plantation Road before dissipating in an area of water and marsh south of Heron Island Way. Path lenth was 2.48 miles and path width was 150 yards. It is possible the tornado moved onshore after forming just west or northwest of Rockville.
Buoy 41008 reported a 35 knot wind gust.
A 911 call center reported flood waters entering a home along W Jones Ave due to heavy rain. Time estimated via radar data.
A 911 call center reported flood waters entering a home along Hodges Circle due to heavy rain. Time estimated via radar data.
A 911 call center reported flood waters entering a home along Turkey Trail due to heavy rain. Time estimated via radar data.
Emergency manager reported a dam failure of a 30 acre pond which connects to the Ohoopee River due to heavy rain.
A gust to 56 mph was observed at the Grays Reef Buoy as line of showers moved over the buoy. A Gale Warning was in effect.
A wind gust to 47 knots/54 mph was observed with shower activity as it moved over the Charleston Pilot Buoy.
WxFlow station at Folly Beach South End observed a gust of 36 kts.
CORMP CHR60 buoy observed a 37 kt wind gust.
